methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

data.narrative:
Groq is a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Jonathan Ross, currently valued at $7.0B with $3.89B raised to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and valuation tier, supplemented by 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR). The peer set includes Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), Crusoe Energy ($10.0B, Series E), Neuralink ($9.7B, private), and Wayve ($8.6B, Series D).

Peer-median analysis is the primary applicable triangulation method. The median valuation across the 8-peer cohort is $9.2B, with a range spanning $2.0B to $15.0B. This method directly reflects market pricing for comparably-staged AI infrastructure and software companies, anchoring the valuation estimate to observable market transactions.

The triangulated valuation range is $7.8B (low) to $10.5B (high), with a median of $9.2B. The current $7.0B valuation sits modestly below the peer median, suggesting modest upside to fair value, though within expected variance for stage and capital intensity. Key uncertainties include limited visibility into unit economics relative to peers and the single-method basis of this analysis; additional approaches (revenue multiple, discounted cash flow) would require operating metrics not provided here.
